workItem.title: Guarantee cleanup of subprocesses created by orchestration tests
workItem.body:

## Context

After the complete Symphony gate passed, the X1 still contained multiple shell processes blocked on test FIFOs and a hung git push for several hours. The processes were created by orchestration restart and audit-volume tests and had become reparented to init, consuming resources and obscuring runtime diagnostics.

## Expected outcome

Every Symphony test that starts shell, Git, Codex, FIFO or helper processes terminates and reaps them on success, failure, timeout and test-process interruption; the complete gate leaves no matching subprocesses behind.

## Scope

- Audit process-spawning tests and helpers
- Add on_exit cleanup with bounded TERM then KILL behavior
- Avoid indefinitely blocking FIFO readers and network pushes
- Add a deterministic leak assertion after the affected test groups
- Document the process cleanup invariant

## Out of scope

- Kill unrelated user processes
- Replace Git or shell-based integration coverage with mocks

## Acceptance criteria

- Restart and workspace tests leave no FIFO reader processes after completion
- Audit-volume tests cannot leave a git push process after timeout or failure
- Cleanup runs for passing, failing and timed-out tests
- make all passes
- A post-gate process scan proves no test-owned children remain
